The Export System is responsible for producing a complete engineering review package for OPSCORE.
Its purpose is not only to export source code, but also to preserve engineering knowledge, architecture, documentation, and AI context.
Export should make a new engineer or a new AI session understand the project without guessing.
Copies generated books into the export package.
OKE exists to preserve engineering knowledge.
OKE is the Engineering Operating System that understands, documents, audits, and improves OPSCORE.
When uncertain, inspect the exported knowledge before modifying source code.

OKE and OPSCORE are separate systems.
OKE is the Engineering Operating System responsible for understanding, documenting, validating, and improving OPSCORE.
OKE assists engineers before, during, and after engineering changes.
Neither system should duplicate the other's responsibility.
Every engineering decision made inside OPSCORE should become engineering knowledge inside OKE.
OKE should continuously become better at understanding OPSCORE without requiring engineers to repeatedly explain the project.
The goal is to preserve engineering knowledge across engineers, AI assistants, devices, and future versions of the system.
Assist engineers by understanding, analyzing, documenting, protecting, and improving software architecture.
Build a portable Engineering Review Package.
Enable any engineer or AI to understand OKE before making changes.

- Teaching AI how to understand projects before modifying them

OKE and OPSCORE are separate systems.
OKE is the Engineering Operating System responsible for understanding, documenting, validating, and improving OPSCORE.
OKE assists engineers before, during, and after engineering changes.
Neither system should duplicate the other's responsibility.
Every engineering decision made inside OPSCORE should become engineering knowledge inside OKE.
OKE should continuously become better at understanding OPSCORE without requiring engineers to repeatedly explain the project.
The goal is to preserve engineering knowledge across engineers, AI assistants, devices, and future versions of the system.
Assist engineers by understanding, analyzing, documenting, protecting, and improving software architecture.
